method_atom: a low-dimensional hierarchy of coupled nonlinear ordinary differential equations that transfers a conserved quantity across successive scales, producing anomalous multiscaling of moment statistics; the dynamics select two generic classes of singular solutions — self-similar structures propagating from coarse to fine scales that build up burstiness/intermittency, and complex-time singularities that arrest the transfer and induce chaos — with a robust numerical method to detect and track both, and a critical value of a coupling-mixture control parameter separating chaotic from regular regimes as a resolution/scale-separation parameter tends to infinity.
object_atom: fully developed hydrodynamic turbulence and its energy cascade, modeled by the mixed Obukhov-Novikov shell model (compared against the complex GOY shell model), studied in the infinite-Reynolds-number limit.
brief_reason: the scale-cascade dynamical machinery with self-similar and complex-time singularities and a critical control-parameter transition is transferable, while the turbulent energy cascade and its shell-model representation is the fluid-dynamics-specific object.
